skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Create polished static visual artwork as PNG or PDF files by first defining a design philosophy, then expressing it through composition, typography, color, and form.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through design philosophy, canvas creation, and visual refinement for static artwork.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Most static high-risk findings are false positives caused by license text in bundled font license files and Apache License text. The markdown backticks around ./canvas-fonts are not shell execution, but SKILL.md does instruct agents to download fonts as needed and embeds a fabricated prior-user refinement statement. No evidence found of credential access, data exfiltration, destructive commands, or malicious prompt-injection authority claims.

medium_findings.title: Unbounded External Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md tells the agent to download whatever fonts are needed. This can cause arbitrary network access and ingestion of untrusted font files, even though the skill also includes bundled fonts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and semantically permits network downloads. I did not find evidence that it exfiltrates data or targets a malicious domain.

low_findings.title: Static License Text Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and hardcoded URL hits appear in Apache and SIL Open Font License text. These are legal license terms and static reference URLs, not executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review showed these lines are standard license prose. The same pattern repeats across bundled OFL files, with no executable code path.

low_findings.title: Markdown Path Misread As Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command finding at SKILL.md line 108 is a markdown inline path reference to ./canvas-fonts. It is not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is prose about typography and font selection. No command substitution syntax or executable script context is present.

low_findings.title: Fabricated Prior User Preference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md asserts that the user already requested museum-level perfection. This can bias an agent by inventing prior user intent, but it does not grant system authority or request unsafe actions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The statement clearly claims a prior user request that may not exist. It is a content-integrity concern, but I did not find prompt text that asks the evaluator to skip review or override system instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Open-Ended Network Resource Acquisition
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits downloading unspecified fonts instead of restricting use to the bundled canvas-fonts directory. This should be constrained before broad publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risk is based on an explicit instruction to download fonts. The impact depends on the host agent permissions and runtime controls.

content.value_statement: Static visuals can look generic when design direction is vague. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create polished PNG or PDF artwork.
actual_capabilities: Creates a written design philosophy before producing visual output.
Guides single-page PNG or PDF artwork creation.
Uses bundled font files from the canvas-fonts directory.
Emphasizes sparse text, spatial composition, color, and form.
Supports multi-page visual series when the user requests them.
Adds a refinement pass focused on polish and composition.
limitations: Requires an AI environment that can create PNG or PDF files.
May suggest font downloads unless constrained to bundled fonts.
Focuses on static visual art, not interactive interfaces or animation.
Does not verify copyright clearance for user-supplied references.

use_cases.title: Create Exhibition Posters
use_cases.description: Turn a theme or event concept into a refined poster with minimal text and strong visual direction.

use_cases.title: Develop Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Produce a high-quality static visual for a launch, announcement, or social campaign.

use_cases.title: Design Editorial Art Pages
use_cases.description: Create magazine-style abstract pages that communicate through composition, typography, and color.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single static design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a single-page PNG poster about [topic]. Use a refined visual philosophy, minimal text, and a limited color palette.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Mood Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a visual direction tied to a brand or mood.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PDF artwork for [brand or project]. Build the design around [mood], [audience], and [key phrase].

prompt_templates.title: Subtle Reference Piece
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that hides a conceptual reference inside abstract design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PNG artwork that subtly references [niche subject]. Make the reference discoverable through form, color, rhythm, and composition.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Visual Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for related pages with varied compositions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-page PDF visual series for [theme]. Each page should share one design philosophy but use a distinct composition.

output_examples.input: Create a poster for an independent film night about memory and distance.
output_examples.output: A design philosophy document and a single-page poster with sparse typography, restrained color, and layered abstract forms.

output_examples.input: Design three pages for a coffee table book about urban silence.
output: A concise visual philosophy for the series.
Three related PDF pages with different compositions.
A final refinement pass focused on alignment and visual balance.

best_practices: Provide a clear subject, mood, format, and intended audience.
Ask the agent to use bundled fonts unless external downloads are approved.
Review generated text carefully because the skill favors visual communication over explanation.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for long-form documents or text-heavy reports.
Do not request imitation of a living artist or copyrighted visual style.
Do not allow unrestricted font downloads in locked-down environments.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates static visual artwork as PNG or PDF files, supported by a short design philosophy.

faq.question: Can it create multiple pages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for multi-page PDF or PNG series when the user requests them.

faq.question: Does it include fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. The package includes a canvas-fonts directory with bundled font files and license text.

faq.question: Is it safe to allow font downloads?
faq.answer: Use caution. Restrict the skill to bundled fonts unless the environment permits network access.

faq.question: Is this skill for user interface design?
faq.answer: No. It is optimized for static art, posters, editorial visuals, and PDF or PNG canvas work.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
